_comment: Master settings file for Project Majdal pipeline. Edit these values before running the notebook.

scale_defaults._comment: Default values applied to every scale when the field is missing from the source JSON. Edit these to match your population and environment. These values are the single source of truth for population/environment/reading level/response format — scales.json should not duplicate them unless a per-scale override is genuinely required.

phase_01_behavioral_domains._comment: Behavioral domain generation AND validation in a single phase. Generates observable behavioral examples then validates each against construct, face, and discriminant validity. LEVEL = likelihood of occurrence in target environment, not item extremity.

min_examples_per_construct.description: Minimum behavioral examples required per construct before a warning is raised

construct_validity.description: LLM must judge the example as genuinely reflecting the target construct

face_validity.description: LLM must judge the example as appearing relevant to target respondents

discriminant_validity.description: LLM must judge the example as distinct from excluded constructs

min_valid_examples_per_scale.description: Minimum valid examples per scale after validation; below this raises a warning

human_review_columns.human_review_pass: Boolean. Default True. Set False in Excel to exclude an example before Phase 2.
human_review_columns.human_comments: Free text. Reason for any override.

phase_02_item_generation._comment: Dominance-item generation from validated behavioral examples. Items are stratified by keying direction (positively vs. negatively keyed) rather than by location on a trait continuum. A roughly 2:1 positive-to-negative ratio is recommended to control acquiescence bias without over-weighting method-factor variance from negatively keyed items.

items_per_keying._comment: Number of items to generate per behavioral domain for each keying direction. positive = standard monotone dominance items (higher trait → stronger endorsement). negative = genuine semantic reversals (lower trait → stronger endorsement), not avoidance or low-intensity statements.

phase_02_item_generation._comment_duplicate_detection: Semantic similarity via Sentence-BERT catches paraphrase duplicates that lexical TF-IDF misses (e.g., 'I move on once a task feels done' / 'I move forward once a task feels complete'). Threshold 0.80 calibrated for SBERT short-item similarity; lexical TF-IDF would use ~0.85.

duplicate_removal.description: Near-duplicate items (cosine similarity >= threshold) are flagged within each keying direction

max_word_count.description: Items exceeding this word count are flagged for review

no_negations_positive_only.description: Positively keyed items containing negation patterns are flagged. Negatively keyed items are permitted (and expected) to use negation and reversal language.

human_review_columns.human_review_pass: Boolean. Default True. Set False to exclude an item before readability analysis. Phase 3 reads this column. Any edits to item_text are also carried forward.
human_review_columns.human_comments: Free text. Notes on obvious construct contamination or generation failure.

phase_03_readability_bias._comment: Readability analysis, LLM simplification, and LLM bias review. Bias is evaluated by multiple LLM reviewers using a structured rubric aligned with Zieky (2013) and Lee et al. (2025).

phase_04_content_validity._comment: Multi-LLM SME content validity review using correspondence (c-value) and distinctiveness (d-value) indices following Hinkin & Tracey (1999) as operationalized by Colquitt et al. (2019) and implemented in the LM-AIG framework (Lee, Son, & Jia, 2025).

agreement_metrics.c_value: Correspondence index: average definitional correspondence rating for the target construct, normalized to 0-1 (Colquitt et al., 2019)
agreement_metrics.d_value: Distinctiveness index: average of (target rating - orbiting rating) / (a - 1), where a = number of anchors on the rating scale (Colquitt et al., 2019)
agreement_metrics.scale_mapping_agreement: proportion of SMEs whose best-rated scale matches the intended/target scale (diagnostic)
agreement_metrics.mean_target_rating: average target-scale correspondence rating across all SMEs (diagnostic)

pass_thresholds._comment: c-value and d-value thresholds from Colquitt et al. (2019). An item passes CV only if both c-value >= 0.88 AND d-value >= 0.35. scale_mapping_agreement is retained as a diagnostic metric.

phase_04_content_validity.prompt_template:
You are a psychometric subject matter expert reviewing items for a psychological assessment.

TARGET SCALE: {scale_name}

ALL SCALES (the item could potentially belong to any of these):
{all_scales_block}

ITEM: "{item_text}"

TASK: Rate how well this item corresponds to EACH of the scales listed above on a 1-7 scale:
  1 = Not at all correspondent
  4 = Moderately correspondent
  7 = Completely correspondent

Also provide a brief rationale (1-2 sentences) for the target scale rating.

Respond in this exact JSON format:
{{"ratings": {{"<scale_name_1>": <1-7>, "<scale_name_2>": <1-7>, ...}}, "rationale": "<text>"}}

human_review_columns.cv_pass: System-set Boolean. True if both c-value >= min_c_value AND d-value >= min_d_value thresholds are met.
human_review_columns.human_review_pass: Boolean. Default True. Overrides cv_pass. Phase 5 reads this column. Any edits to item_text are also carried forward.
human_review_columns.human_comments: Free text. Especially useful when c-value or d-value is borderline.

phase_05_pseudo_factor_analysis._comment: Semantic PFA using sentence transformers as a proxy for item correlations, followed by final item pool assembly and human review package. transformer_models is a list: if multiple models are specified, their cosine similarity matrices are averaged before factor analysis. Reversal for embedding is driven by the item_keying flag from Phase 2 — no heuristic detection is required.
phase_05_pseudo_factor_analysis._comment_encoding_strategy: atomic = standard; negatively keyed items embedded as written. atomic_reversed = flip negatively keyed items to their positive-pole paraphrase before embedding (recommended for NEO-type instruments; Guenole et al. 2025). both = run both strategies and report Tucker's congruence for each scale. Based on Guenole et al. (2025): atomic-reversed achieved 94% factor recovery on NEO vs 71% with standard atomic; standard atomic achieved 98% on HEXACO. For unknown instruments, run both and choose per scale.

reversal_rewrite._comment: LLM call used to paraphrase negatively keyed items to their positive-pole equivalents for the atomic_reversed encoding strategy. Triggered deterministically by item_keying == 'negative'; no heuristic detection step is used. Keep temperature at 0 for reproducibility.

phase_05_pseudo_factor_analysis._factor_extraction_method_comment: Factor extraction method passed to FactorAnalyzer. Valid values: 'principal' (PCA-style, default — matches Guenole et al. 2025 convention), 'minres' (EFA, minimum residual / OLS), 'ml' (EFA, maximum likelihood). The reported RMSR fit metric is unaffected by this choice; loading patterns will differ slightly between methods on the same similarity matrix.

Rule_5_Cross_Sample_Viable.description: All-items loading >= 0.20 (item loads adequately on the all-items single factor — cross-keying viable)

Rule_6_Keying_Viable.description: Within-keying loading >= 0.25 (item loads adequately within its own keying stratum — positive items vs. negative items analysed separately)

model_fit_thresholds._comment: RMSR is the only reliable fit metric for PFA (no sample size). See Suarez-Alvarez et al. (2026).

discriminant_validity._comment: ratio: target loading / max cross-loading must exceed threshold. ceiling: absolute max cross-loading regardless of ratio (soft flag). Both configurable.

human_review_columns.human_comments: Free text. Especially important for DAAL failures and discriminant flags.
human_review_columns.human_notes: Free text. Rationale, suggested rewording, or regeneration flags.
